title: Yosys crashes on reading exported verilog design containing concatenation.
body:
Having run `opt -full` after `flatten` yosys crashes on importing just exported verilog file.

The error message is:

> 1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend.
Parsing Verilog input from `multimux_out_2.v' to AST representation.
ERROR: Parser error in line multimux_out_2.v:34: syntax error, unexpected '['


## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
read_verilog multimux.v
hierarchy -auto-top -check;
proc; clean;
memory;
opt -full;
flatten;

write_verilog multimux_out_1.v
opt -full;
write_verilog multimux_out_2.v

delete;
read_verilog multimux_out_2.v
```

## Expected behavior

Yosys does not crash on multimux_out_1.v.

## Actual behavior
Yosys crashes on multimux_out_2.v

The difference between the generated verilog is that \m3.q is now concatenated and accessed through indexing in the same line:

`assign \m3.q  = { \m2.q , \m1.q  }[select[0] +: 1];`

Yosys does still generate  `assign q_tmp = { \m2.q , \m1.q  };`   
but does not use it.

If I insert q_tmp into the assignment of \m3.q it works again.

The input file and the two output files I got are attached.

[multimux.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2962973/multimux.zip)
